channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Persistent strong sewer smell outside early in the morning, 20th July
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to report a persistent and severe sewage smell around the postcode DA17 6EF. This issue has been happening every day for a week now. I have already reported this to Thames Water, but I am also notifying the council as it affects the local environment. Here are the details of the issue: * When: Every morning, specifically between 3:00 AM and 6:00 AM. * Duration: It has been recurring for the past week. * Where: Outside the property around DA17 6EF. It is completely coming from the outside (not from inside my house). * Description: The smell is very strong, similar to an open manhole or cesspit. Because it happens during the dark early hours, I have not been able to identify the exact manhole or source on the street. However, the odour is strong enough to wake me up or prevent me from opening the windows. Could you please investigate this environmental/air pollution issue in the area during these hours? Thank you for your assistance. Best regards,
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lower Park Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hole opening up in road and water leaking from under road, 20th July
item.description:
Hole opening up in road and water leaking from under road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Logs Hill, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Victoria Way & Woolwich Road - sweeping required, 20th July
item.description:
Hi there, There is a lot of litter - please ensure weekly sweeping happens on these streets this week. Best

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Way, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping at the bottom of Victoria Way, 20th July
item.description:
Hi there, Someone decided to dump on the pavement their artificial Christmas tree - please collect it. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Way, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping - bottom of Victoria Way SE7, 20th July
item.description:
Hi there, There is constant fly-tipping next to that council block, please collect and send a notice to the residents as this is not acceptable. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Victoria Way, Charlton

item.title: Green waste left in grey bags on the pavement, 20th July
item.description:
Hi there, Someone trimmed the bushes on Victoria Way next to Bowen Drive and left many grey bags - can you please collect them asap. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bowen Drive, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags have been here at least 3 days., 20th July
item.description:
Please collect 2 x piles of blue street-cleaning bags (leaves). They will start "attracting" other rubbish v.soon. Been here since Weds/Thurs. Now Sunday. The street cleaners work hard to keep our area nice and we love where we live. Pls collect ASAP.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Kellerton Road, Hither Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken paving slabs at 39, 43 & 55 Elmstead Ave, 20th July
item.description:
Can these broken paving slabs be inspected please?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmstead Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent street cleaning and anti-social behaviour issue, 20th July
item.description:
There is a serious hygiene, safety, and public hazard issue at this location right beneath our residential building. We have young children here, and they cannot safely enter or leave the building because of drunken individuals constantly using this secluded/blind spot at midnight to urinate and defecate. The area is left extremely dirty, foul-smelling, and unsafe for families. This anti-social behaviour is creating an unacceptable risk to our children's well-being. We urgently demand a deep street cleaning, and immediate action from the council to install fencing, secure gates, better lighting, or CCTV to permanently close off this hidden area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Fore Street, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Strong urine smell, 20th July
item.description:
Continuous public urination by Oliver Goldsmith Estate behind and by shops on High Street on Marmont Road. Really strong, unpleasant urine smell.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckham High Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti., 20th July
item.description:
Some names are engraved on wall need to be fixed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rotherhithe New Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish everywhere, 110 - 180 Sandy Hill Road, SE18 7BA, 19th July
item.description:
I wpuld like to report dirt and rubbish on Sandy Hill Road - upper part. The road definitely is not cleaned once a week. There are piles of cans, cardboard boxes, glass all along the road and pavement between numbers 110 - 180. Pure embarrassment.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sandy Hill Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Branch knocked off, 19th July
item.description:
Branch knocked off tree outside no. 93 probably by delivery van & lying on grass verge
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Lane, Chislehurst, Greater London

item.title: Bags of rubbish on pavement outside 92 Powerscroft Road, 19th July
item.description:
Bags of rubbish on pavement outside 92 Powerscroft Road, possibly linked to more bags of rubbish on the corner of Powerscroft Rd and Lawley Street. Recurring problem.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Powerscroft Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 19th July
item.description:
Hazards waste including gas canisters

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeated commercial waste management failures at SK Buy N Buy, including leaking food waste, offensive odours, food debris, littering, and suspected improper disposal of meat-contaminated liquid into road drainage. These recurring issues are creating a significant environmental health nuisance, attracting pests, contaminating the public highway, and interfering with residents’ enjoyment of their homes. Urgent investigation and enforcement action are requested., 19th July
item.description:
I wish to report what I believe are repeated breaches of commercial waste management and environmental health requirements by SK Buy N Buy. Commercial food waste is regularly left awaiting collection, including boxes containing raw meat placed on top of foul-smelling bins. Following waste collection, food debris and foul-smelling liquid are left on the public highway, contaminating the carriageway. The odour is so severe that it prevents me from opening my windows without gagging, substantially interfering with the enjoyment of my home. The shop’s litter is also frequently dispersed across the surrounding street, and I have personally observed buckets containing what appeared to be meat-contaminated liquid being emptied into the road drainage system. I request that the Council investigate whether this disposal complies with the Environmental Protection Act 1990, the Environmental Protection (Duty of Care) Regulations, and all relevant environmental health and waste disposal requirements. These are not isolated incidents but recurring problems that have become significantly worse in recent weeks. I request an urgent investigation, inspection of the business’s waste management practices, cleansing of the affected highway, and appropriate enforcement action where breaches are identified.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Penrose Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 19th July
item.description:
Flytipping of bed/mattress sometime during the evening of 19 july

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 51-59, Lawrie Park Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

item.title: Baby swing hinges loose, 19th July
item.description:

item.title: Foam flytipped, 19th July
item.description:
There is a load of foam fly tipped. This is in addition to the mattress that has been reported. The mattress has made it look like a dumping spot and now there are more things there. Please remove so others don’t try and dump more here. Thank you

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Griffin Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
